data.title: Chiho Environmental Group Adds Two Executive Directors to Bolster Strategy and Finance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Chiho Environmental Group Limited ( (HK:0976) ).

Chiho Environmental Group Limited has strengthened its senior management team with the appointment of two new executive directors, both effective 30 April 2026. The move underscores the company’s efforts to deepen its financial, investment and strategic capabilities as it navigates growth and capital allocation in the environmental services sector.

The board has named Chu Yuejiang, an experienced financial and corporate manager formerly with Pengxin International Mining, as an executive director and member of its executive, strategy and investment, and pricing committees. It has also appointed investment and M&A specialist Yao Xiang, previously a director and vice president at Shanghai-listed Loncin Motor, as an executive director joining the executive, nomination, strategy and investment, and pricing committees.

Both Chu and Yao have signed one-year service agreements starting 30 April 2026 with monthly remuneration of HK$50,000, subject to annual review by the board and remuneration committee. Their appointments, initially lasting until the next annual general meeting when shareholders will vote on re-election, are expected to bolster Chiho’s governance, financial oversight and deal-making capabilities, potentially supporting future expansion and portfolio optimisation.

More about Chiho Environmental Group Limited

Chiho Environmental Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company focused on environmental services, including recycling and related resource management activities. The group operates in the broader environmental and sustainability sector, serving industrial and commercial clients seeking waste treatment, materials recovery, and environmentally responsible processing solutions in key markets including Greater China.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$690.2M

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co. Ltd. ( (HK:1643) ) has issued an update.

Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co., Ltd. has announced a further delay in sending a shareholder circular related to a major transaction involving the acquisition of sale shares, originally expected by 30 April 2026. The company now expects to dispatch the circular by 30 June 2026, citing the need for additional time to finalise pro forma financial information for the enlarged group.

To accommodate the delay, the company has applied to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange for a waiver from strict compliance with the listing rule governing the timing of circular dispatch. As the waiver has not yet been granted, the company has indicated it will issue further announcements as appropriate, signaling ongoing regulatory engagement and a potential shift in the timetable for completing the acquisition.

More about Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co. Ltd.

Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and engaged in the modern Chinese medicine sector. The group operates through subsidiaries and is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical corporate governance structure for a listed pharmaceutical or healthcare enterprise.

An announcement from Datang Environment Industry Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1272) ) is now available.

Datang Environment Industry Group has appointed Xu Guang as its new general manager for the term from 30 April 2026 to 31 December 2027, with remuneration set at a basic annual salary of RMB220,000 plus performance-linked components. Upon his appointment taking effect, chairman Zhu Liming will cease acting as general manager, ending an interim management arrangement that has been in place since September 2023.

Xu, a senior engineer and long-time executive within various Datang Group entities, currently serves as deputy party secretary of the company and has held multiple leadership, discipline inspection, and labour union roles across Datang’s power generation, research, and regional energy development arms. His elevation to general manager is expected to reinforce continuity of leadership and strengthen alignment with the parent group’s governance and operational framework, potentially supporting stability and strategic execution for stakeholders.

data.title: Jintai Energy Details Audit Delays Tied to Convertible Notes and Lunar New Year Disruptions
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jintai Energy Holdings Limited ( (HK:2728) ) has shared an update.

Jintai Energy Holdings has issued a supplemental announcement explaining the delay in publishing its audited annual results and report for the year ended 31 December 2025, as well as the continued suspension of trading in its shares. The company highlights that unresolved accounting treatment for matured convertible notes, which are now classified as current liabilities repayable on demand, is central to the delay and has material implications for its going concern and liquidity assessment.

Management is still negotiating new agreements with the noteholder to amend key terms and possibly extend the notes, and the auditors require more time to assess different scenarios for the group’s cash flow forecast. The audit has also been slowed by delayed bank, supplier, and customer confirmations and outstanding supporting documents due in part to the Lunar New Year holiday, and the company says it will start post‑year‑end audit work earlier in future to avoid similar disruptions.

More about Jintai Energy Holdings Limited

Jintai Energy Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The group operates in the energy sector and conducts its business mainly through subsidiaries in the People’s Republic of China, dealing with banks, suppliers, and customers across this market.

Average Trading Volume: 7,736,648

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

An announcement from JOINN Laboratories (China) Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6127) ) is now available.

JOINN Laboratories (China) Co., Ltd. has granted 1,430,000 H-share awards, equal to about 1.20% of its issued H shares and 0.191% of total share capital, to 12 selected participants under its existing share incentive scheme. data.title: Sinco Pharmaceuticals Chairman Resigns, Triggering Temporary Governance Non-Compliance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sinco Pharmaceuticals Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:6833) ) has provided an announcement.

Sinco Pharmaceuticals Holdings Limited announced that chairman and executive director Huang Xiangbin has resigned from his roles, including as chairman of the nomination committee and authorised representative, effective 30 April 2026 to focus on other commitments. Vice chairman and executive director Huang Zhijian has been appointed as the new authorised representative, while the company temporarily falls out of compliance with Hong Kong Listing Rule 3.27A because the nomination committee currently lacks a chairman, and the board has committed to filling the position within three months and issuing further updates.

The resignation is not due to any disagreement with the board, and Sinco’s directors expressed gratitude for Huang Xiangbin’s contributions and extended a warm welcome to Huang Zhijian in his expanded role. The governance gap around the nomination committee chair highlights a short-term compliance issue for the company, but management has pledged best efforts to restore full adherence to listing requirements promptly, a move closely watched by shareholders and regulators concerned with board oversight and corporate governance standards.

More about Sinco Pharmaceuticals Holdings Ltd.

Sinco Pharmaceuticals Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, operating in the pharmaceutical sector. The company is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors and is subject to Hong Kong Listing Rules for corporate governance and board committee structures.

data.title: Giant Biogene Moves to Replace EY With KPMG as Auditor
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Giant Biogene Holding Co. Ltd. ( (HK:2367) ) just unveiled an update.

Giant Biogene Holding Co. Ltd. has launched a planned change of auditors as part of its corporate governance review, deciding not to reappoint Ernst & Young after seven consecutive years of auditing its consolidated financial statements. EY will complete the audit for the year ended 31 December 2025 and retire at the conclusion of the upcoming annual general meeting, with the company stressing that the move will not affect the 2025 audit.

The board, following the audit committee’s recommendation, will seek shareholder approval at the AGM to appoint KPMG as the new auditor from the end of that meeting until the next annual general meeting. The audit committee cited KPMG’s scale, technical competence, independence, resources, and alignment with regulatory guidance as key reasons for its selection, and both EY and the board confirmed there are no disagreements or issues requiring shareholder attention, indicating a smooth transition with no expected disruption to the 2026 audit timetable.

data.title: Prada lifts Q1 2026 revenues 14% as Americas, Asia drive growth
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Prada SpA ( (HK:1913) ) has provided an update.

Prada S.p.A. reported unaudited net revenues of €1.43 billion for the first quarter of 2026, a 14% year-on-year increase at constant exchange rates, supported by retail sales of €1.25 billion and solid wholesale and royalty growth. The Prada brand delivered a resilient quarter with steady full-price sales, while Miu Miu maintained growth despite tough comparisons and Middle East disruption, and recently consolidated Versace contributed €143 million in net revenues.

Regional performance was mixed, with mid-teens growth in the Americas reflecting recent investments, robust expansion in Asia Pacific led by Greater China and Korea, and a steady outcome in Japan after a reallocation of Hawaii, offset by weaker trends in Europe and a sharp decline in the Middle East. The shift toward full-price retail and reduced outlet contribution, combined with accelerating wholesale and royalties, suggests Prada is navigating geopolitical and macroeconomic headwinds while reinforcing its positioning in key luxury markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1913) stock is a Hold with a HK$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Prada SpA stock, see the HK:1913 Stock Forecast page.

More about Prada SpA

Prada S.p.A. is a global luxury fashion group based in Italy, operating in the high-end apparel, leather goods, footwear, and accessories markets. The company’s primary brands include Prada, Miu Miu, Versace, and Church’s, with a strong retail presence across Asia Pacific, Europe, the Americas, Japan, and the Middle East.

data.title: Radiance Holdings’ Auditor Ernst & Young to Retire After Going-Concern Disclaimer
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited ( (HK:9993) ).

Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited has announced that Ernst & Young will retire as its auditor following the conclusion of the upcoming annual general meeting, after the firm decided not to seek reappointment due to considerations such as audit fees and internal resource allocation. While Ernst & Young previously issued no opinion on the group’s 2025 consolidated financial statements because of insufficient evidence regarding going concern assumptions, both the board and the audit committee stressed there is no disagreement or other matters requiring shareholder attention, and the company plans to appoint a new auditor in accordance with listing requirements.

The departure of a Big Four auditor, especially following a year in which it was unable to opine on the accounts over going-concern doubts, may raise investor questions about Radiance Holdings’ financial robustness and risk profile. Management’s assurance of an orderly search for a replacement audit firm and its expression of gratitude to Ernst & Young are aimed at containing governance concerns, but the eventual choice of new auditor and any future audit opinions will be closely watched by shareholders and creditors.

More about Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited

Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 9993, operates within the broader Chinese property and investment sector. The company is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure aimed at oversight and compliance with local listing rules.

data.title: AMCO United Holding Sets June 2026 AGM, Seeks 20% Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

AMCO United Holding ( (HK:0630) ) just unveiled an announcement.

AMCO United Holding has called its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will consider the adoption of the 2025 audited consolidated financial statements and re-election of three directors, including one executive and two independent non-executive directors. Investors will also vote on the re-appointment of CCTH CPA Limited as auditor and a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital, a move that preserves financial flexibility for potential fundraising and corporate actions while subjecting future dilution to defined limits and shareholder oversight.

More about AMCO United Holding

AMCO United Holding Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group operates as an investment holding company, with its shares traded under stock code 630, and is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

Average Trading Volume: 155,327

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$290.3M

data.title: Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) Details Board and Committee Structure
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) ( (HK:0444) ).

Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) Limited has confirmed the composition of its board, led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Mr. Zhang Xiaoliang, along with two other executive directors, one non-executive director and two independent non-executive directors. The company has also detailed the membership of its four key board committees—Audit, Remuneration, Nomination and Investment—clarifying leadership roles and responsibilities in a move that reinforces its corporate governance framework and oversight structure for stakeholders.

The Audit Committee is composed of independent non-executive directors Mr. Yu Zhenxin and Mr. Zong Hao, underscoring independent financial oversight. The Remuneration, Nomination and Investment Committees are each chaired by a mix of independent and executive directors, which may help balance management influence and independent judgment in decisions on pay, board appointments and capital deployment.

More about Sincere Watch (Hong Kong)

Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company in the watch and timepiece sector, incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical listed-company governance structure in the region.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$60.44M
